metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4063
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1 and S1: Increase in synaptic neuropeptide in Myopic-overexpressing and -deficient motoneurons is not accounted for by altered DCV content, transport, or bouton size, but is consistent with an increase in synaptic DCV number.

To check if the increase in neuropeptide storage stems from decreased neuropeptide secretion, the paper investigates if PTPN23 mutation/ overexpression causes an accumulation of older DCVs in boutons.

Figure 2: Neuropeptide-timer construct [atrial natriuretic factor tagged with monomeric Kusabira Green Orange (ANF-mK-GO) driven by 386Y-GAL4] that converts from green to red fluorescence over hours was used. Green/red fluorescence ratios showed that PTPN23 knockdown or overexpression each produced older DCVs compared with control animals.

Figure 3D and E: PTPN23 heterozygous mutant (T482/P) showed a mild change in fluorescence intensity over time for neuropeptide Dilp2-GFP indicating that PTPN23 might inhibit DCV exocytosis.

Figure 3F and G: PTPN23 overexpression completely inhibits DCV exocytosis.

Figure 4A-D: Neuropeptide release was induced by tonic depolarization induced by application of elevated extracellular K+. Even in strong depolarising conditions, PTPN23 RNAi and overexpression reduced the neuropeptide release significantly.

Figure 4E-H: cAMP-evoked synaptic neuropeptide release was also inhibited dramatically by both RNAi and overexpression of PTPN23. This indicates that PTPN23 directly regulates DCV secretion machinery rather than an indirect regulatory effect via voltage-gated Ca2+ channel.

The 'regulate' box was ticked on the basis of the residual neuropeptide release observed in PTPN23 KO (Figure 3, D and E) indicating that it regulates the process.. Experimental description: Dilp2-GFP was driven directly by the native neuronal synaptobrevin promoter to label DCVs in transgenic flies. This line was further modified by recombination with OK6-GAL4, which was used to drive UAS-Mop RNAi and overexpression constructs in motor neurons.
For controls, UAS lines were crossed to the nsyb-Dilp2-GFP line without a GAL4 driver.
For mop mutant studies, nsyb-Dilp2-GFP was crossed to w*; mopT482 P{neoFRT}80B/TM6B,Tb to generate a stock of w; nsyb-Dilp2-GFP/CyO; mopT482 P{neoFRT}80B/TM6B. This stock was then crossed to the mop allele y w; P{EPgy2}mopEY06090, and non-Tb larvae with the mutant combination of mopT482/P{EPgy2}mopEY06090 were selected for analysis.
Experiments to determine the age of DCVs were done by using ANF tagged fluorescent timer protein monomeric mK-GO.
